description: Gaussian damping parameter (sigma) sensitivity sweep for PME QM/MM embedding
system: B5 (QM water in TIP3P box, 3.0 nm cubic, 2652 atoms, 884 SOL)
motivation: Reviewer items R1-Minor-3 (sigma sensitivity), R2-Optional-6 (sweep sigma), R1-Optional-5 (drift vs sigma plot). Justify choice of sigma=0.1 nm as default.

sigma_0p05.note: Elevated block ratio (>3) suggests non-stationary drift; first-half drift 5× second-half

sigma_0p10.note: Default value; drift consistent with A-5 PBC regression 3.0nm

sigma_0p15.note: Highest drift of the four; still well within threshold

sigma_0p20.note: Smoothest QM energy (lowest fluctuations) but largest deviation from PME reference

key_findings.qm_energy_trend: QM energy shifts ~10 kJ/mol per 0.05 nm sigma increase (less negative with larger sigma = weaker QM-MM coupling)
key_findings.qm_fluctuation_trend: sigma_QM decreases monotonically: 15.86 -> 13.36 -> 7.11 -> 4.34 kJ/mol (larger sigma = smoother embedding potential)
key_findings.drift_non_monotonic: Drift peaks at sigma=0.15 (7.04e-04), lowest at sigma=0.05 (3.40e-04); no simple drift-vs-sigma trend
key_findings.sigma_0p10_justification: sigma=0.10 nm balances embedding fidelity (QM energy within 1.3 kJ/mol of PME reference) with stable NVE dynamics (block ratio 1.45, 0 SCC failures)
